title: Sleeve for a filter assembly
abstract: A filter assembly (10) includes a housing (12) with an inlet (26) and an outlet (28). The filter assembly (10) also includes a tubular filter element (14) located downstream of the inlet (26) and upstream of the outlet (28) and a sleeve element (16, 150) surrounding and blocking at least a portion of the filter element (14).
claims: Claims Claims 1. A filter assembly (10), comprising a housing (12) including an inlet (26) and an outlet (28); a tubular filter element (14) located downstream of the inlet (26) and upstream of the outlet (28); and a sleeve element (16, 150) surrounding and blocking at least a portion of the filter element (14). 2. The filter assembly (10) of claim 1, further including an adhesive coupling the sleeve element (16, 150) around an exterior of the tubular filter element (14). 3. The filter assembly (10) of claim 2, wherein the sleeve element (16, 150) surrounds approximately 50% of the tubular filter element (14). 4. The filter assembly (10) of claim 2, wherein the sleeve element (16, 150) forms at least one opening (46, 152) between the sleeve element (16, 150) and a portion of the housing (12), and wherein the at least one opening (46, 152) is positioned on a side opposite to the inlet (26). 5. The filter assembly (10) of any one preceding claim, wherein the sleeve element (16, 150) includes a plurality of openings (152) around the sleeve element (16, 150). 6. The filter assembly (10) of claim 5, wherein the plurality of openings (152) are arranged in groups of through-holes (154), and wherein the through-holes (152) of each group of through-holes (154) increase in size from one end of the sleeve element (16, 150) to another end of the sleeve element (16, 150). 7. The filter assembly (10) of claim 6, wherein the through- holes (152) of each group of through-holes (154) increase in size in a direction away from the inlet (26). 8. The filter assembly (10) of claim 7, wherein the through- holes (152) of each group of through-holes (154) increase by approximately 30% in the direction away from the inlet (26). 9. The filter assembly (10) of claim 7, wherein the sleeve element (16, 150) is circular, and wherein each group of through-holes (154) is spaced approximately 45 degrees apart around a circumference of the sleeve element (16, 150). 10. The filter assembly (10) of any one preceding claim, wherein the sleeve element (16, 150) is formed of an impermeable material.
